sloka_667.meaning: O Droṇāchārya, best of the twice-born

sloka_676.meaning: O lord of the earth, Dhṛtarāṣṭra

sloka_692.meaning: O descendant of the Vṛṣṇi dynasty
when the family is overcome by irreligion
when the women of the family become corrupt

sloka_666.meaning: and the powerful King of Kāśī

sloka_674.meaning: the performer of mighty tasks, Bhīmasen (sounded)

sloka_665.meaning: army of seven akṣauhiṇīs (one akṣauhiṇī is 21,870 chariots, 21,870 elephants, 65,610 horses, and 109,350 foot-soldiers)

sloka_687.meaning: of what value is our kingdom
and what is the need of its royal pleasures, or life itself
kingdom with its pleasures and enjoyable objects

sloka_663.meaning: at the holy land of Kurukṣetra

sloka_678.meaning: having seen the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra arrayed before him ready for battle

sloka_680.meaning: by Arjuna, the conqueror of sleep

sloka_683.meaning: and my mouth is becoming dry

sloka_691.meaning: With the decline of the dynasty

sloka_694.meaning: of the destroyers of the dynasty

sloka_690.meaning: the evil of destroying the dynasty?
their hearts being afflicted with greed

sloka_693.meaning: for both the dynasty and its destroyers
due to the cessation of obsequial offerings of food and water

sloka_675.meaning: King Yudhiṣṭhir, the son of Kuntī
the conchshells named Sughoṣa and Maṇipuṣpaka

sloka_688.meaning: not to speak of ruling the earth?
for the sovereignty of even the three worlds

sloka_686.meaning: nor the enjoyment of a kingdom

sloka_668.meaning: Kṛpa, who is victorious in battle
and prepared to sacrifice their lives

sloka_670.meaning: at all points of entrance into the formation

sloka_696.meaning: Out of greed for royal pleasures

sloka_672.word: śaṅkhāḥ cha bheryaḥ cha paṇava-ānaka gomukhāḥ
sloka_672.meaning: conchshells, kettledrums, hand-drums, bugles, and other instruments
